title: API Misuse, Oracles, Side Channels, and Defensive Boundaries · Guided Systems Studio
summary: A product-security pair incrementally builds cryptographic misuse and oracle threat review, changes one attacker-controlled input, and keeps earlier Go, accessibility, secret-lifecycle, testing, and evidence invariants active. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of cryptographic misuse and oracle threat review while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Identify padding, validity, format, key, user, replay, decompression, and timing oracles across direct and indirect observations.
Bound lengths, counts, nesting, KDF work, signature sets, certificate paths, retries, concurrency, memory, CPU, and deadlines before expensive cryptography.
Keep useful internal typed causes and safe telemetry while exposing uniform bounded failures and terminal cleanup to untrusted callers.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Crypto Go Oracle Classification
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop cryptographic engineering team handles case 1114 during a credential migration window. Build deterministic Go evidence to bound attacker-controlled cryptographic work and move oracle and side-channel claims to native evidence; record key custody, terminal failure, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk ownership; then rotate the active key while old data remains readable and new writes cannot downgrade. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Identify padding, validity, format, key, user, replay, decompression, and timing oracles across direct and indirect observations.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Cryptography and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Crypto Go Oracle Classification.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for identify padding, validity, format, key, user, replay, decompression, and timing oracles across direct and indirect observations. For Cryptography and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Crypto Go Oracle Classification, keep Crypto Go Oracle Classification connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

content.text: A workshop cryptographic engineering team handles case 5971 during a certificate rollover incident. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. steps.title: Cryptography and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Crypto Go Constant Time Transfer
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: crypto-go-constant-time-transfer-bqwmjw" that uses case 6271 to bound attacker-controlled cryptographic work and move oracle and side-channel claims to native evidence. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import cryptographic packages, generate or handle production keys, open sockets, contact TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, compliance services, or external effects, read host state, execute host commands, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5 compile, standard crypto APIs, authoritative vectors, fuzz, race, native side-channel measurement, interoperability, controlled network and key-custody systems, load, fault, recovery, compliance, and production gates.
